name: Apply run and per-scenario limits
prompt:
An AXIS project exists in this directory. Apply two limits:

1. Add an OVERALL run cap of 30 minutes to `axis.config.json`. When this limit is hit, every remaining and in-progress job should be aborted.
2. Add a per-scenario cap to `scenarios/long-task.json` ONLY: 5 minutes and 50,000 tokens.

Use the existing AXIS limits configuration shape — do not invent new field names. Do not modify any unrelated fields.

rubric.check: `axis.config.json` still parses as valid JSON and preserves the original `scenarios` and `agents` fields
`scenarios/long-task.json` still parses as valid JSON and preserves its `name`, `prompt`, and `rubric` fields
The scenario file has a top-level `limits` object with `time_minutes: 5` and `tokens: 50000`
The agent did NOT use any invented limit field names like `timeoutMinutes`, `maxTokens`, or `tokenLimit`

variants.name: with-docs
variants.prompt:
An AXIS project exists in this directory. Apply two limits:

1. Add an OVERALL run cap of 30 minutes to `axis.config.json`. When this limit is hit, every remaining and in-progress job should be aborted.
2. Add a per-scenario cap to `scenarios/long-task.json` ONLY: 5 minutes and 50,000 tokens.

Use the existing AXIS limits configuration shape — do not invent new field names. Do not modify any unrelated fields.

Reference the AXIS documentation at https://axisproject.ai/configuration for the limits configuration shape and where each level (run vs. scenario) is placed.
